records.ety: [OE. lurken, lorken, prob. a dim. from the source of E. lower to frown. See Lower, and cf. Lurch, a sudden roll, Lurch to lurk.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Lurked (lrkt); p. pr. & vb. n. Lurking.]

defs: To lie hidden; to lie in wait.
quotes: Like wild beasts, lurking in loathsome den.
Let us . . . lurk privily for the innocent.
The defendant lurks and wanders about in Berks.
